verses.text: A trespass against a neighbour — deceit, robbery, lying — brings guilt. Against man. Sin against others is sin against God.
A trespass against a neighbour — deceit, robbery, lying — brings guilt. Against man. Sin against others is sin against God.
A trespass against a neighbour — deceit, robbery, lying — brings guilt. Against man. Sin against others is sin against God.
He restores it with a fifth added and brings a ram; he is forgiven. Restitution. Repentance restores and atones.

The law of the burnt offering: it burns on the altar all night. The offering. Devotion ascends through the night.
The law of the burnt offering: it burns on the altar all night. The offering. Devotion ascends through the night.
The law of the burnt offering: it burns on the altar all night. The offering. Devotion ascends through the night.
The fire on the altar is never to go out. Ever burning. Devotion must never be quenched.

The law of the meat offering, eaten by the priests in the holy place. The priests' bread. Those who serve the altar are fed by it.

The priest's own meat offering is wholly burnt, not eaten. Wholly given. The priest keeps nothing for himself.

The law of the sin offering, most holy, eaten by the offering priest. Most holy. Atonement's flesh is sacred.
The law of the sin offering, most holy, eaten by the offering priest. Most holy. Atonement's flesh is sacred.

Whatever touches it is holy; the blood-stained garment is washed. Holy. The sin offering hallows all it touches.
